title: Combining Quantization and PEFT
concept: QLoRA takes LoRA further by quantizing the frozen backbone model to 4-bit precision, while training the 16-bit LoRA adapter layers on top of it.

exercises.prompt: QLoRA is a training technique that combines which two concepts?
exercises.explain: QLoRA is an advanced Parameter-Efficient Fine-Tuning (PEFT) method that combines model quantization with adapter-based tuning.

cards.title: Squeezing Giant Models
cards.body: Large Language Models are absolute memory hogs. What if you could shrink the main model to a fraction of its size, but still train it to learn new tricks without losing any accuracy? That is the superpower of QLoRA!

cards.title: What is QLoRA?
cards.body: QLoRA (Quantized Low-Rank Adaptation) is a technique that quantizes a pre-trained LLM down to 4-bit precision to save massive amounts of memory, while adding a small set of trainable 16-bit LoRA adapters to keep the model smart and adaptable.

cards.title: The Sticky Note Encyclopedia
cards.body: Imagine buying a massive encyclopedia (the frozen LLM). Instead of writing your notes directly in it, you attach tiny sticky notes (LoRA adapters) on the pages. To save bookshelf space, you compress the heavy encyclopedia into a microfiche film (4-bit quantization) but still write your sticky notes on normal paper with a regular pen (16-bit precision) so they are easy to read and edit.

cards.title: QLoRA in Action
cards.body: If you want to fine-tune a massive 65-billion parameter model, you would normally need multiple expensive high-end GPUs. With QLoRA, the massive model's footprint is shrunk so drastically that you can perform the entire fine-tuning process on a single professional GPU!

cards.title: Myth: 4-Bit Means Lower Quality
cards.body: People often think that shrinking a model to 4-bit precision makes it dumb. Thanks to QLoRA's clever design (like NormalFloat data types and double quantization), the model retains virtually all of its original performance while using a fraction of the memory.

cards.title: The QLoRA Split
cards.body: See how QLoRA splits the workload to give you the best of both worlds: extreme memory savings and high-precision learning.

right.text: Kept at 16-bit BrainFloat/Float precision to ensure stable, high-quality updates.

left.text: Compressed to 4-bit NormalFloat precision to save maximum memory.

exercises.prompt: What is the main advantage of using QLoRA over traditional fine-tuning?

options.text: It makes the model train much slower.
It drastically reduces the GPU memory (VRAM) required for fine-tuning.
It deletes the base model entirely.
It prevents the model from learning new facts.

exercises.explain: QLoRA allows us to train very large models on consumer-grade or single-GPU setups by drastically lowering the GPU VRAM requirements.

exercises.prompt: True or False: In QLoRA, the quantized 4-bit base model weights remain frozen during training.
exercises.explain: During QLoRA training, the 4-bit base model is kept frozen (static), and only the small 16-bit LoRA adapter weights are updated.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the missing word to complete the description of QLoRA's dual strategy.
exercises.sentence: QLoRA achieves its tiny memory footprint by combining base model [blank] with lightweight trainable adapters.
exercises.explain: QLoRA combines quantization (shrinking the base model to 4-bit) with PEFT (using LoRA adapters) to achieve high performance on limited hardware.

exercises.prompt: An AI developer configures a QLoRA training run with a 4-bit quantized base model and a 32-bit adapter. Identify the correct evaluation of this configuration:

options.text: This represents standard LoRA without quantization.
This is incorrect because QLoRA adapters are kept at 16-bit precision, not 32-bit.

exercises.explain: QLoRA uses 4-bit precision for the base model weights, and 16-bit precision for the active adapter weights. A 32-bit adapter is not standard for QLoRA.

exercises.prompt: An AI engineer wants to deploy QLoRA to fine-tune a model on a single GPU. How are the precision levels distributed between the base model and the adapters?

options.text: The base model weights are compressed to 4-bit, and the adapter weights are kept at 16-bit.
Both the base model and the adapters are compressed to 4-bit precision.
The base model is kept at 16-bit, and only the adapters are compressed to 4-bit.
Both the base model and adapters are kept at full 32-bit floating point precision.

exercises.explain: QLoRA applies 4-bit quantization specifically to the frozen base model (backbone) to minimize GPU memory usage, while keeping the active LoRA adapter weights at 16-bit precision for high-quality learning.
